description_long: This is a multi-tiered pagoda structure with five distinct stories, each separated by wide, upward-curving eaves and supported by prominent wooden beams. The exterior features a combination of orange-red wooden framework and white infill panels, with intricate railings and latticework on each level. The base is broad and square, with a central staircase leading up to the main entrance, flanked by symmetrical balconies and railings. Each story is slightly smaller than the one below, creating a tapered silhouette that culminates in a large, ornate finial at the apex. The rooflines are gracefully curved, typical of East Asian architecture, and the overall design is highly symmetrical and decorative. The structure is elevated on a stone or concrete platform, suggesting durability and prominence in an urban or park setting.
description: A five-story, orange-red and white pagoda with upward-curving eaves, ornate balconies, and a decorative finial atop a tiered roof structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central staircase, main entrance, and symmetrical balconies with detailed railings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered eaves, side balconies, and the depth of the structure's wooden framework.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear view of the back balconies and the symmetry of the tiered roofs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the side balconies and the ornate finial at the top.
